mex-cdmx.name: Mexico City
mex-cdmx.description: The metropolis that pulses with football. The only city to host three World Cup opening matches.

stadium.name: Azteca Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Stage of Pele's goals in 70 and Maradona's 'Hand of God' in 86.

travel.transport: Efficient and affordable Metro and Metrobus system.

dishes.name: Tacos al Pastor
dishes.description: Marinated pork with pineapple and onions.

dishes.name: Chiles en Nogada
dishes.description: Stuffed peppers with walnut sauce and pomegranate.

dishes.name: Mole Poblano
dishes.description: Complex sauce made with chili and chocolate.

attractions.name: Zocalo
attractions.description: The historic heart of the city.

attractions.name: Chapultepec Forest
attractions.description: One of the largest urban parks in the world.

trivia: Azteca Stadium will be the first to host three opening matches.
The city sinks about 4-12 inches per year.
It has the highest number of museums in the Americas.
mex-cdmx.economics: Primary financial and cultural hub of Latin America. Est. GDP $500B.

usa-ny.name: New York / New Jersey
usa-ny.description: The capital of the world and home to the Grand Final on July 19, 2026.

stadium.name: MetLife Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Home to two NFL teams and venue for the Copa America Centenario final.

travel.transport: 24h subway network in NY and NJ Transit trains.

dishes.name: Bagels with Lox
dishes.description: Smoked salmon and cream cheese on a bagel.

dishes.name: Cheesesteak (NJ Style)
dishes.description: Steak with provolone on a hoagie roll.

attractions.name: Times Square
attractions.description: The brightest intersection in the world.

attractions.name: Central Park
attractions.description: Green oasis in the middle of the concrete jungle.

trivia: MetLife Stadium will be renamed for the final due to FIFA rules.
Over 800 languages are spoken in Greater NY.
NY has more skyscrapers than any other global city.
usa-ny.economics: Global financial center (Wall Street). Est. GDP $2.3 Trillion.

usa-la.name: Los Angeles
usa-la.description: Cinema, beaches, and innovation. The entertainment capital.

stadium.name: SoFi Stadium
stadium.historicFact: The world's most expensive stadium, featuring an epic 360-degree screen.

attractions.name: Griffith Observatory
attractions.description: Panoramic view of the sign and the city.

attractions.name: Santa Monica Pier
attractions.description: Classic amusement park by the sea.

trivia: SoFi Stadium's roof is translucent to allow natural light.
LA will host the Olympics in 2028 for the third time.
The Hollywood sign originally said 'Hollywoodland'.
usa-la.economics: Entertainment and tech hub (Silicon Beach). Est. GDP $1.1 Trillion.

usa-dal.name: Dallas
usa-dal.description: Texan metropolis that will host the highest number of matches (9 games).

stadium.name: AT&T Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Known as 'Jerry World', featuring one of the largest screens on the planet.

travel.transport: Rental car necessary. DART light rail to downtown.

dishes.name: Tex-Mex Fajitas
dishes.description: Grilled meat with peppers and tortillas.

attractions.name: Dealey Plaza
attractions.description: Historic site of the JFK assassination.

trivia: The stadium screen is so large it had to be a primary design consideration.
Dallas will have the largest fan transportation hub.
Many games will be indoors due to the Texas heat.
usa-dal.economics: Logistics, telecommunications, and energy hub. Est. GDP $600B.

usa-atl.name: Atlanta
usa-atl.description: Jewel of the South, home of Coca-Cola and civil rights.

stadium.name: Mercedes-Benz Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Innovative retractable roof that opens like a camera lens.

travel.transport: MARTA links airport to downtown simply.

attractions.name: Georgia Aquarium
attractions.description: One of the world's largest with whale sharks.

attractions.name: Martin Luther King Jr. Park
attractions.description: History of the civil rights movement.

trivia: The retractable roof opens in just 8 minutes.
Atlanta's airport is the busiest in the world.
Host of the 1996 Olympic Games.
usa-atl.economics: Global transportation and media hub. Est. GDP $450B.

usa-mia.name: Miami
usa-mia.description: Latin gateway, party, sun, and the Messi effect.

stadium.name: Hard Rock Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Traditional Super Bowl venue and now a 2026 World Cup host.

travel.transport: Free Metromover in downtown. Rental car recommended.

dishes.name: Key Lime Pie
dishes.description: Tangy lime pie typical of Florida.

trivia: Miami has the world's largest collection of Art Deco architecture.
It is the only major US city founded by a woman.
Known as the cruise capital of the world.
usa-mia.economics: International tourism and finance hub. Est. GDP $350B.

can-tor.name: Toronto
can-tor.description: The most diverse metropolis in the world and economic engine of Canada.

stadium.name: BMO Field
stadium.historicFact: Home of the Canadian national team, being expanded for the World Cup.

travel.transport: TTC (Subway/Streetcars) is excellent. UP Express from airport.

dishes.name: Poutine
dishes.description: Fries with cheese curds and gravy.

dishes.name: Peameal Bacon
dishes.description: Cured loin bacon sandwich rolled in cornmeal.

attractions.name: CN Tower
attractions.description: Iconic structure with a glass floor.

attractions.name: Distillery District
attractions.description: Victorian village with art and dining.

attractions.name: Royal Ontario Museum
attractions.description: Natural history and world culture museum.

trivia: Over 180 languages and dialects are spoken in Toronto.
The PATH network is the world's largest underground shopping gallery.
Over 50% of residents were born outside Canada.

can-van.name: Vancouver
can-van.description: Where mountains meet the Pacific Ocean. Moderate climate.

stadium.name: BC Place
stadium.historicFact: Hosted the 2015 Women's World Cup final and 2010 Winter Olympics.

travel.transport: SkyTrain links airport to downtown automatically.

dishes.name: Butter Chicken
dishes.description: Reflecting the strong South Asian community.

attractions.name: Stanley Park
attractions.description: Green heart of the city surrounded by sea.

attractions.name: Capilano Bridge
attractions.description: Epic suspension bridge over a canyon.

trivia: Stanley Park is larger than NYC's Central Park.
Known as 'Hollywood North' for the film industry.
Rarely snows heavily in the downtown area.
can-van.economics: Tech, film, and maritime trade hub. Est. GDP $150B.

mex-mty.name: Monterrey
mex-mty.description: Mexico's industrial engine surrounded by surreal mountains.

stadium.name: BBVA Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Known as the 'Steel Giant' with a stunning mountain view.

travel.transport: Taxis and Uber are common. Metrorrey available.

attractions.name: Macroplaza
attractions.description: One of the world's largest urban plazas.

attractions.name: Chipinque Park
attractions.description: Trails in the Sierra Madre with city views.

trivia: The BBVA Stadium is considered one of the most beautiful in the world.
Monterrey has the highest GDP per capita in Mexico.
Cerro de la Silla is an iconic natural monument.
mex-mty.economics: Industrial capital of Mexico (steel, glass). Est. GDP $140B.

mex-gdl.name: Guadalajara
mex-gdl.description: The soul of Mexico: Land of Mariachi and Tequila.

stadium.name: Akron Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Its architecture resembles a volcano crowned by a cloud.

travel.transport: Light rail and official taxis are the best options.

trivia: Mariachi and Tequila were born in this region.
The stadium is solar-powered.
Hosted matches in the 1970 and 1986 World Cups.
mex-gdl.economics: Cultural, tech, and agro-industrial hub. Est. GDP $100B.

usa-hou.name: Houston
usa-hou.description: Space, energy, and diversity. The 'Space City'.

stadium.name: NRG Stadium
stadium.historicFact: The first NFL stadium with a functional retractable roof.

travel.transport: Car is essential. METRORail serves downtown.

dishes.name: Crawfish Boil
dishes.description: Cajun-style boiled crawfish with corn and potatoes.

attractions.name: NASA Space Center
attractions.description: Official visitor center and mission control.

attractions.name: Museum District
attractions.description: Complex with 19 art and science museums.

trivia: Houston is the most ethnically diverse city in the US.
Center of NASA's famous Apollo missions.
Home to the world's largest medical center.
usa-hou.economics: Energy capital of the world (oil and gas). Est. GDP $500B.
usa-hou.weather: June/July: Average 34°C / 24°C. High heat and humidity.

usa-kc.name: Kansas City
usa-kc.description: The passionate heart of the USA and world capital of BBQ.

stadium.name: Arrowhead Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Guinness World Record for stadium noise (142.2 dB).

travel.transport: Car recommended. Buses link key points.

dishes.name: Loose Meat Sandwich
dishes.description: Spiced ground beef classic on a bun.

attractions.name: 18th & Vine Jazz District
attractions.description: Birthplace of jazz and negro leagues museum.

trivia: Known as the 'City of Fountains' (more than Rome).
The city spans across two states (MO and KS).
The invention of 'Burnt Ends' happened here.
usa-kc.economics: Agro-industrial and health tech hub. Est. GDP $150B.

usa-phi.name: Philadelphia
usa-phi.description: Birthplace of American democracy and history of independence.

stadium.name: Lincoln Financial Field
stadium.historicFact: 100% green energy generated by onsite solar panels.

travel.transport: SEPTA (Trains and subway) is very functional.

dishes.name: Philly Cheesesteak
dishes.description: Steak, cheese, and onions on Italian bread.

attractions.name: Independence Hall
attractions.description: Where the Declaration of Independence was signed.

trivia: Philadelphia was the first capital of the United States.
It has the most outdoor murals of any US city.
Home to the Liberty Bell.
usa-phi.economics: Education, healthcare, and high-tech manufacturing center. Est. GDP $480B.

usa-sea.name: Seattle
usa-sea.description: Lush nature, coffee, and technology in the Pacific Northwest.

stadium.name: Lumen Field
stadium.historicFact: European-style soccer atmosphere with an insanely vibrating crowd.

travel.transport: Excellent Link Light Rail between airport and downtown.

dishes.name: Seattle Style Hot Dog
dishes.description: Hot dog with cream cheese and onions.

trivia: Birthplace of Grunge music (Nirvana, Pearl Jam).
First major US city to legalize ridesharing.
Seattle consumes more coffee than any other US city.
usa-sea.economics: Cloud computing, aerospace, and maritime export hub. $440B GDP.

usa-sf.name: San Francisco
usa-sf.description: In the heart of Silicon Valley, where the future is created.

stadium.name: Levi's Stadium
stadium.historicFact: One of the world's most technological and eco-friendly stadiums.

travel.transport: BART and Caltrain link the Bay Area. Uber/Lyft available.

dishes.name: Dungeness Crab
dishes.description: Fresh local crab from the Pacific.

attractions.name: Golden Gate Bridge
attractions.description: The most photographed bridge in the world.

attractions.name: Alcatraz
attractions.description: Famous deactivated prison in the bay.

trivia: The Golden Gate Bridge is constantly painted international orange.
The city was built on hills and buried ships.
Silicon Valley is the global epicentro of innovation.
usa-sf.economics: Global innovation and venture capital epicenter. Est. $600B GDP.

usa-bos.name: Boston
usa-bos.description: Academic and sporting tradition, the soul of New England.

stadium.name: Gillette Stadium
stadium.historicFact: Stage of Tom Brady's glories and now a 2026 World Cup hub.

travel.transport: The 'T' is the oldest subway in the US and very practical.

attractions.name: Fenway Park
attractions.description: The oldest baseball stadium in the US.

trivia: Boston opened the first public school in the US in 1635.
The Boston subway was the first in the country (1897).
Known for the 'Big Dig', one of the largest road projects.
usa-bos.economics: Biotech, elite education, and finance. Est. $500B GDP.
